title: TCP Connections, Accept Loops, Half-Closes, Deadlines, and Failure · Go Semantics Case: Half Close Reset Eof
summary: A raw server treats TCP as messages, leaks accepted connections, uses no deadlines, retries ambiguous writes, and cannot distinguish peer close, timeout, reset, or local shutdown. Planned artifact: Add and verify the half close reset eof increment in bounded TCP connection lifecycle state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish orderly EOF, CloseRead, CloseWrite, full close, reset, timeout, and local shutdown effects on protocol completeness.
Set and refresh absolute read and write deadlines by protocol phase and classify timeout evidence without assuming cancellation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Half Close Reset Eof · Theory Half Close Reset Eof
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 6063 in a fuzzing harness varying every legal input segmentation. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify connection, deadline, completion, and close state without inventing message boundaries; preserve this operating constraint: one parser state transition may consume bytes only after its full precondition holds; then lower one byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs before allocation or dispatch. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish orderly EOF, CloseRead, CloseWrite, full close, reset, timeout, and local shutdown effects on protocol completeness.
